description: Letter from Natan b. Yehuda of Alexandria to Sason b. Meshullam, a judge of Cairo. The letter contains many personal matters that reflect the milieu of the political elite in Alexandria and Cairo. The letter reveals important details on the life of Avraham b. Yaʿaqov Darʿī (unless this is a different al-Darʿī? Natan b. Yehuda was active in the mid-12th century, while Avraham b. Yaʿaqov Darʿī was active ca. 1100). On al-Darʿī's deathbed, he accused the writer of having sent a messenger to intercept a medical prescription and swap it with a counterfeit prescription, hence murdering him. Apparently, the person who served as the representative of Mevorakh b. Saadya (referred to in the letter as 'the rayyis Abū l-Faḍl) in Alexandria after the year 1094 died in the end of the first decade of the twelfth century due to a sudden disease. The letter quotes a lament of Shemuel ha-Nagid. (Information from Frenkel)
tags: illness: foul play, Illness: treatment, illness letter 969-1517, personal, Illness
description_he: מכתב מנתן ב' יהודה מאלכסנדריה לססון ב' משולם, שופט בקהיר. המכתב חושף פרטים על חיי אברהם ב' יעקב דרעי; על ערש דווי האשים אותו דרעי שהחליף מרשם רפואי במרשם מזויף וכך גרם למותו. המכתב מצטט קינה של שמואל הנגיד.
